title: Benefit-Risk Assessment — QBRI & QBR
description: Quantitative benefit-risk assessment tools. Two complementary families: QBRI (expert-judgment, 7-variable formula) and QBR (statistical-evidence from contingency tables using PRR/ROR/IC/EBGM). Includes therapeutic window computation via Hill curve integration. Pure Rust — no network calls.

tools.name: compute-qbri
tools.description: Compute the Quantitative Benefit-Risk Index (QBRI) from benefit and risk parameters. QBRI = (B × Pb × Ub) / (R × Pr × Sr × Tr). Returns index value, decision (Approve/Monitor/More Data/Reject), and confidence. Use after gathering trial efficacy data and post-market safety signals.

parameters.name: benefit_effect
parameters.description: Benefit effect size (e.g., hazard ratio, odds ratio from clinical trial)

parameters.name: benefit_pvalue
parameters.description: P-value from clinical trial efficacy endpoint (0-1)

parameters.name: unmet_need
parameters.description: Unmet medical need score (1-10, where 10 = no existing treatment)

parameters.name: risk_signal
parameters.description: Risk signal strength (e.g., PRR or ROR value from FAERS)

parameters.name: risk_probability
parameters.description: Causal probability (0-1, from Naranjo or WHO-UMC assessment)

parameters.name: risk_severity
parameters.description: Severity on Hartwig-Siegel scale (1=mild to 7=fatal)

parameters.name: reversible
parameters.description: Whether the adverse event is reversible (default: true)

tools.name: derive-qbri-thresholds
tools.description: Derive optimal QBRI decision thresholds (tau_approve, tau_monitor, tau_uncertain) from historical FDA drug approval decisions. Returns optimized thresholds with accuracy metrics.

parameters.name: use_synthetic
parameters.description: Use synthetic test data based on 8 well-known drugs (default: true)

tools.name: qbri-equation
tools.description: Get the QBRI equation with variable definitions and hypothesis thresholds. Reference tool — explains what each term means.

tools.name: compute-qbr
tools.description: Compute full statistical benefit-risk ratio from contingency tables using disproportionality analysis (PRR/ROR/IC/EBGM). Supports simple, Bayesian, composite, and therapeutic window forms. Use with real FAERS contingency data.

parameters.name: method
parameters.description: Disproportionality method: prr, ror, ic, or ebgm

parameters.name: benefit_tables
parameters.description: Benefit contingency tables, each with {a, b, c, d} counts

parameters.name: risk_tables
parameters.description: Risk contingency tables, each with {a, b, c, d} counts

parameters.name: benefit_weights
parameters.description: Optional weights for benefit tables [{value, confidence}]

parameters.name: risk_weights
parameters.description: Optional weights for risk tables [{value, confidence}]

parameters.name: hill_efficacy
parameters.description: Optional Hill curve params for efficacy {k_half, n_hill}

parameters.name: hill_toxicity
parameters.description: Optional Hill curve params for toxicity {k_half, n_hill}

parameters.name: integration_bounds
parameters.description: Optional dose range for therapeutic window {dose_min, dose_max, intervals}

tools.name: compute-qbr-simple
tools.description: Quick benefit-risk ratio from one benefit and one risk contingency table. Returns the B/R ratio, individual signal strengths, and the method used. Simplest entry point for benefit-risk assessment.

parameters.name: method
parameters.description: Disproportionality method: prr, ror, ic, or ebgm

parameters.name: benefit_table
parameters.description: Benefit contingency table {a, b, c, d}

parameters.name: risk_table
parameters.description: Risk contingency table {a, b, c, d}

tools.name: compute-therapeutic-window
tools.description: Compute therapeutic window from efficacy and toxicity dose-response curves using Hill equation integration. Returns the benefit-over-risk AUC ratio across a dose range. Wider window = safer drug.

parameters.name: efficacy
parameters.description: Efficacy Hill curve: {k_half: EC50 dose, n_hill: Hill coefficient}

parameters.name: toxicity
parameters.description: Toxicity Hill curve: {k_half: TC50 dose, n_hill: Hill coefficient}

parameters.name: bounds
parameters.description: Optional dose range: {dose_min, dose_max, intervals}. Defaults to 0.1-100 with 1000 intervals.
